subject: [RFC PATCH 26/45] mm: page_alloc: prevent UNMOVABLE/RECLAIMABLE mixing in pageblocks

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch prevents the mixing of UNMOVABLE and RECLAIMABLE page allocations within a single pageblock, which can lead to internal fragmentation and wasted memory. The issue arises when free pages of different types are not distinguishable by the buddy allocator's compatibility heuristic. To address this, the patch introduces a new helper function to detect cross-type pairs and tightens two paths in the page allocator: try_to_claim_block() and __rmqueue_steal(). The changes ensure that UNMOVABLE and RECLAIMABLE pages are not mixed within a pageblock, allowing for more efficient memory usage. The test plan involves building and rebooting on a bare-metal devvm with existing tainted SPBs to verify the improvements in free distribution across different order sizes.
